- autoplan: Auto-review pipeline — reads the full CEO, design, eng, and DX review skills from disk and runs them sequentially with auto-decisions using 6 decision principles. (gstack)
- benchmark: Performance regression detection using the browse daemon. (gstack)
- benchmark-models: Cross-model benchmark for gstack skills. (gstack)
- browse: Fast headless browser for QA testing and site dogfooding. (gstack)
- canary: Post-deploy canary monitoring. (gstack)
- careful: Safety guardrails for destructive commands. (gstack)
- codex: OpenAI Codex CLI wrapper — three modes. (gstack)
- connect-chrome: Launch GStack Browser — AI-controlled Chromium with the sidebar extension baked in.
Opens a visible browser window where you can watch every action in real time.
The sidebar shows a live activity feed and chat. Anti-bot stealth built in.
Use when asked to "open gstack browser", "launch browser", "connect chrome",
"open chrome", "real browser", "launch chrome", "side panel", or "control my browser".
Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "show me the browser".
- context-restore: Restore working context saved earlier by /context-save. (gstack)
- context-save: Save working context. (gstack)
- cso: Chief Security Officer mode. (gstack)
- design-consultation: Design consultation: understands your product, researches the landscape, proposes a complete design system (aesthetic, typography, color, layout, spacing, motion), and generates font+color preview... (gstack)
- design-html: Design finalization: generates production-quality Pretext-native HTML/CSS. (gstack)
- design-review: Designer's eye QA: finds visual inconsistency, spacing issues, hierarchy problems, AI slop patterns, and slow interactions — then fixes them. (gstack)
- design-shotgun: Design shotgun: generate multiple AI design variants, open a comparison board, collect structured feedback, and iterate. (gstack)
- devex-review: Live developer experience audit. (gstack)
- document-generate: Generate missing documentation from scratch for a feature, module, or entire project. (gstack)
- document-release: Post-ship documentation update. (gstack)
- freeze: Restrict file edits to a specific directory for the session. (gstack)
- gstack: Fast headless browser for QA testing and site dogfooding. (gstack)
- gstack-upgrade: Upgrade gstack to the latest version.
- guard: Full safety mode: destructive command warnings + directory-scoped edits. (gstack)
- health: Code quality dashboard. (gstack)
- investigate: Systematic debugging with root cause investigation. (gstack)
- ios-clean: Remove the DebugBridge SPM package and all
- ios-design-review: Visual design audit for iOS apps on real hardware. (gstack)
- ios-fix: Autonomous iOS bug fixer. (gstack)
- ios-qa: Live-device iOS QA for SwiftUI apps. (gstack)
- ios-sync: Regenerate the iOS debug bridge against the latest upstream gstack templates. (gstack)
- land-and-deploy: Land and deploy workflow. (gstack)
- landing-report: Read-only queue dashboard for workspace-aware ship. (gstack)
- learn: Manage project learnings.
- make-pdf: Turn any markdown file into a publication-quality PDF. (gstack)
- office-hours: YC Office Hours — two modes. (gstack)
- open-gstack-browser: Launch GStack Browser — AI-controlled Chromium with the sidebar extension baked in.
- pair-agent: Pair a remote AI agent with your browser. (gstack)
- plan-ceo-review: CEO/founder-mode plan review. (gstack)
- plan-design-review: Designer's eye plan review — interactive, like CEO and Eng review. (gstack)
- plan-devex-review: Interactive developer experience plan review. (gstack)
- plan-eng-review: Eng manager-mode plan review. (gstack)
- plan-tune: Self-tuning question sensitivity + developer psychographic for gstack (v1: observational). (gstack)
- qa: Systematically QA test a web application and fix bugs found. (gstack)
- qa-only: Report-only QA testing. (gstack)
- retro: Weekly engineering retrospective. (gstack)
- review: Pre-landing PR review. (gstack)
- scrape: Pull data from a web page. (gstack)
- setup-browser-cookies: Import cookies from your real Chromium browser into the headless browse session. (gstack)
- setup-deploy: Configure deployment settings for /land-and-deploy.
- setup-gbrain: Set up gbrain for this coding agent: install the CLI, initialize a local PGLite or Supabase brain, register MCP, capture per-remote trust policy. (gstack)
- ship: Ship workflow: detect + merge base branch, run tests, review diff, bump VERSION, update CHANGELOG, commit, push, create PR. (gstack)
- skillify: Codify the most recent successful /scrape flow into a permanent browser-skill on disk. (gstack)
- spec: Turn vague intent into a precise, executable spec in five phases. (gstack)
- sync-gbrain: Keep gbrain current with this repo's code and refresh agent search guidance in CLAUDE.md. Wraps the gstack-gbrain-sync orchestrator with state (gstack)
- unfreeze: Clear the freeze boundary set by /freeze, allowing edits to all directories again. (gstack)
- _gstack-command: Fast headless browser for QA testing and site dogfooding. (gstack)
- claude-hud:setup: Configure claude-hud as your statusline
- claude-hud:configure: Configure HUD display options (layout, presets, display elements) while preserving advanced manual overrides
- episodic-memory:search-conversations: Search previous Claude Code conversations using semantic or text search
- superpowers:brainstorm: Deprecated - use the superpowers:brainstorming skill instead
- superpowers:execute-plan: Deprecated - use the superpowers:executing-plans skill instead
- superpowers:write-plan: Deprecated - use the superpowers:writing-plans skill instead
- ralph-loop:cancel-ralph: Cancel active Ralph Loop
- ralph-loop:ralph-loop: Start Ralph Loop in current session
- ralph-loop:help: Explain Ralph Loop plugin and available commands
- claude-md-management:revise-claude-md: Update CLAUDE.md with learnings from this session
- claude-dashboard:setup: Configure claude-dashboard status line settings
- claude-dashboard:setup-alias: Add check-ai shell alias for quick CLI usage check
- claude-dashboard:check-usage: Check all AI CLI (Claude, Codex, Gemini, z.ai) usage limits and get recommendations
- claude-dashboard:update: Update statusLine path to latest plugin version
- codex:rescue: Delegate investigation, an explicit fix request, or follow-up rescue work to the Codex rescue subagent
- codex:setup: Check whether the local Codex CLI is ready and optionally toggle the stop-time review gate
- everything-claude-code:verify: Legacy slash-entry shim for the verification-loop skill. Prefer the skill directly.
- everything-claude-code:flutter-review: Review Flutter/Dart code for idiomatic patterns, widget best practices, state management, performance, accessibility, and security. Invokes the flutter-reviewer agent.
- everything-claude-code:tdd: Legacy slash-entry shim for the tdd-workflow skill. Prefer the skill directly.
- everything-claude-code:flutter-build: Fix Dart analyzer errors and Flutter build failures incrementally. Invokes the dart-build-resolver agent for minimal, surgical fixes.
- everything-claude-code:skill-health: Show skill portfolio health dashboard with charts and analytics
- everything-claude-code:feature-dev: Guided feature development with codebase understanding and architecture focus
- everything-claude-code:skill-create: Analyze local git history to extract coding patterns and generate SKILL.md files. Local version of the Skill Creator GitHub App.
- everything-claude-code:evolve: Analyze instincts and suggest or generate evolved structures
- everything-claude-code:eval: Legacy slash-entry shim for the eval-harness skill. Prefer the skill directly.
- everything-claude-code:e2e: Legacy slash-entry shim for the e2e-testing skill. Prefer the skill directly.
- everything-claude-code:sessions: Manage Claude Code session history, aliases, and session metadata.
- everything-claude-code:docs: Legacy slash-entry shim for the documentation-lookup skill. Prefer the skill directly.
- everything-claude-code:save-session: Save current session state to a dated file in ~/.claude/session-data/ so work can be resumed in a future session with full context.
- everything-claude-code:devfleet: Legacy slash-entry shim for the claude-devfleet skill. Prefer the skill directly.
- everything-claude-code:santa-loop: Adversarial dual-review convergence loop — two independent model reviewers must both approve before code ships.
- everything-claude-code:rust-test: Enforce TDD workflow for Rust. Write tests first, then implement. Verify 80%+ coverage with cargo-llvm-cov.
- everything-claude-code:learn-eval: Extract reusable patterns from the session, self-evaluate quality before saving, and determine the right save location (Global vs Project).
- everything-claude-code:rust-review: Comprehensive Rust code review for ownership, lifetimes, error handling, unsafe usage, and idiomatic patterns. Invokes the rust-reviewer agent.
- everything-claude-code:kotlin-test: Enforce TDD workflow for Kotlin. Write Kotest tests first, then implement. Verify 80%+ coverage with Kover.
- everything-claude-code:cpp-test: Enforce TDD workflow for C++. Write GoogleTest tests first, then implement. Verify coverage with gcov/lcov.
- everything-claude-code:cpp-review: Comprehensive C++ code review for memory safety, modern C++ idioms, concurrency, and security. Invokes the cpp-reviewer agent.
- everything-claude-code:rust-build: Fix Rust build errors, borrow checker issues, and dependency problems incrementally. Invokes the rust-build-resolver agent for minimal, surgical fixes.
- everything-claude-code:kotlin-review: Comprehensive Kotlin code review for idiomatic patterns, null safety, coroutine safety, and security. Invokes the kotlin-reviewer agent.
- everything-claude-code:cpp-build: Fix C++ build errors, CMake issues, and linker problems incrementally. Invokes the cpp-build-resolver agent for minimal, surgical fixes.
- everything-claude-code:kotlin-build: Fix Kotlin/Gradle build errors, compiler warnings, and dependency issues incrementally. Invokes the kotlin-build-resolver agent for minimal, surgical fixes.
- everything-claude-code:rules-distill: Legacy slash-entry shim for the rules-distill skill. Prefer the skill directly.
- everything-claude-code:context-budget: Legacy slash-entry shim for the context-budget skill. Prefer the skill directly.
- everything-claude-code:jira: Retrieve a Jira ticket, analyze requirements, update status, or add comments. Uses the jira-integration skill and MCP or REST API.
- everything-claude-code:instinct-status: Show learned instincts (project + global) with confidence
- everything-claude-code:review-pr: Comprehensive PR review using specialized agents
- everything-claude-code:resume-session: Load the most recent session file from ~/.claude/session-data/ and resume work with full context from where the last session ended.
- everything-claude-code:instinct-import: Import instincts from file or URL into project/global scope
- everything-claude-code:instinct-export: Export instincts from project/global scope to a file
- everything-claude-code:python-review: Comprehensive Python code review for PEP 8 compliance, type hints, security, and Pythonic idioms. Invokes the python-reviewer agent.
- everything-claude-code:prune: Delete pending instincts older than 30 days that were never promoted
- everything-claude-code:code-review: Code review — local uncommitted changes or GitHub PR (pass PR number/URL for PR mode)
- everything-claude-code:hookify: Create hooks to prevent unwanted behaviors from conversation analysis or explicit instructions
- everything-claude-code:prp-prd: Interactive PRD generator - problem-first, hypothesis-driven product spec with back-and-forth questioning
- everything-claude-code:hookify-list: List all configured hookify rules
- everything-claude-code:hookify-help: Get help with the hookify system
- everything-claude-code:prp-pr: Create a GitHub PR from current branch with unpushed commits — discovers templates, analyzes changes, pushes
- everything-claude-code:hookify-configure: Enable or disable hookify rules interactively
- everything-claude-code:claw: Legacy slash-entry shim for the nanoclaw-repl skill. Prefer the skill directly.
- everything-claude-code:prp-plan: Create comprehensive feature implementation plan with codebase analysis and pattern extraction
- everything-claude-code:prp-implement: Execute an implementation plan with rigorous validation loops
- everything-claude-code:gradle-build: Fix Gradle build errors for Android and KMP projects
- everything-claude-code:aside: Answer a quick side question without interrupting or losing context from the current task. Resume work automatically after answering.
- everything-claude-code:go-test: Enforce TDD workflow for Go. Write table-driven tests first, then implement. Verify 80%+ coverage with go test -cover.
- everything-claude-code:prp-commit: Quick commit with natural language file targeting — describe what to commit in plain English
- everything-claude-code:go-review: Comprehensive Go code review for idiomatic patterns, concurrency safety, error handling, and security. Invokes the go-reviewer agent.
- everything-claude-code:agent-sort: Legacy slash-entry shim for the agent-sort skill. Prefer the skill directly.
- everything-claude-code:prompt-optimize: Legacy slash-entry shim for the prompt-optimizer skill. Prefer the skill directly.
- everything-claude-code:go-build: Fix Go build errors, go vet warnings, and linter issues incrementally. Invokes the go-build-resolver agent for minimal, surgical fixes.
- everything-claude-code:promote: Promote project-scoped instincts to global scope
- everything-claude-code:projects: List known projects and their instinct statistics
- everything-claude-code:flutter-test: Run Flutter/Dart tests, report failures, and incrementally fix test issues. Covers unit, widget, golden, and integration tests.
- everything-claude-code:plan: Restate requirements, assess risks, and create step-by-step implementation plan. WAIT for user CONFIRM before touching any code.
- everything-claude-code:orchestrate: Legacy slash-entry shim for dmux-workflows and autonomous-agent-harness. Prefer the skills directly.
- fe-review-agents:diff-review: 여러 reviewer subagent를 한꺼번에 호출해 git diff를 리뷰하고 synthesizer로 중요도순 종합 리포트를 만듭니다. 사용법 — /fe-review-agents:diff-review [scope] [lang=ko|en] [severity_min=LOW|MED|HIGH|CRITICAL]. scope=staged(기본)/unstaged/branch:<name>/range:<a>..<b>
- fe-review-agents:file-review: 여러 reviewer subagent를 한꺼번에 호출해 단일 파일을 리뷰하고 synthesizer로 중요도순 종합 리포트를 만듭니다. 사용법 — /fe-review-agents:file-review <파일 경로> [lang=ko|en] [severity_min=LOW|MED|HIGH|CRITICAL]
- code-review:code-review: Code review a pull request
- superpowers:writing-skills: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment
- superpowers:writing-plans: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code
- superpowers:verification-before-completion: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always
- superpowers:using-superpowers: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions
- superpowers:receiving-code-review: Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically questionable - requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement or blind implementation
- superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch: Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work - guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup
- superpowers:executing-plans: Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute in a separate session with review checkpoints
- superpowers:using-git-worktrees: Use when starting feature work that needs isolation from current workspace or before executing implementation plans - creates isolated git worktrees with smart directory selection and safety verification
- superpowers:dispatching-parallel-agents: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies
- superpowers:test-driven-development: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code
- superpowers:brainstorming: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation.
- superpowers:systematic-debugging: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes
- superpowers:subagent-driven-development: Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session
- superpowers:requesting-code-review: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements
- claude-session-driver:driving-claude-code-sessions: Use when acting as a project manager that delegates tasks to other Claude Code sessions - launch workers, assign them work, monitor progress, review their tool calls, and collect results
- episodic-memory:remembering-conversations: Use when user asks 'how should I...' or 'what's the best approach...' after exploring code, OR when you've tried to solve something and are stuck, OR for unfamiliar workflows, OR when user references past work. Searches conversation history.

- claude-md-management:claude-md-improver: Audit and improve CLAUDE.md files in repositories. Use when user asks to check, audit, update, improve, or fix CLAUDE.md files. Scans for all CLAUDE.md files, evaluates quality against templates, outputs quality report, then makes targeted updates. Also use when the user mentions "CLAUDE.md maintenance" or "project memory optimization".
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- codex:codex-cli-runtime: Internal helper contract for calling the codex-companion runtime from Claude Code
- codex:gpt-5-4-prompting: Internal guidance for composing Codex and GPT-5.4 prompts for coding, review, diagnosis, and research tasks inside the Codex Claude Code plugin
- codex:codex-result-handling: Internal guidance for presenting Codex helper output back to the user
- everything-claude-code:messages-ops: Evidence-first live messaging workflow for ECC. Use when the user wants to read texts or DMs, recover a recent one-time code, inspect a thread before replying, or prove which message source was actually checked.
- everything-claude-code:mcp-server-patterns: Build MCP servers with Node/TypeScript SDK — tools, resources, prompts, Zod validation, stdio vs Streamable HTTP. Use Context7 or official MCP docs for latest API.
- everything-claude-code:django-security: Django security best practices, authentication, authorization, CSRF protection, SQL injection prevention, XSS prevention, and secure deployment configurations.
- everything-claude-code:market-research: Conduct market research, competitive analysis, investor due diligence, and industry intelligence with source attribution and decision-oriented summaries. Use when the user wants market sizing, competitor comparisons, fund research, technology scans, or research that informs business decisions.
- everything-claude-code:django-patterns: Django architecture patterns, REST API design with DRF, ORM best practices, caching, signals, middleware, and production-grade Django apps.
- everything-claude-code:manim-video: Build reusable Manim explainers for technical concepts, graphs, system diagrams, and product walkthroughs, then hand off to the wider ECC video stack if needed. Use when the user wants a clean animated explainer rather than a generic talking-head script.
- everything-claude-code:design-system: Use this skill to generate or audit design systems, check visual consistency, and review PRs that touch styling.
- everything-claude-code:deployment-patterns: Deployment workflows, CI/CD pipeline patterns, Docker containerization, health checks, rollback strategies, and production readiness checklists for web applications.
- everything-claude-code:logistics-exception-management: Codified expertise for handling freight exceptions, shipment delays, damages, losses, and carrier disputes. Informed by logistics professionals with 15+ years operational experience. Includes escalation protocols, carrier-specific behaviors, claims procedures, and judgment frameworks. Use when handling shipping exceptions, freight claims, delivery issues, or carrier disputes.
- everything-claude-code:defi-amm-security: Security checklist for Solidity AMM contracts, liquidity pools, and swap flows. Covers reentrancy, CEI ordering, donation or inflation attacks, oracle manipulation, slippage, admin controls, and integer math.
- everything-claude-code:deep-research: Multi-source deep research using firecrawl and exa MCPs. Searches the web, synthesizes findings, and delivers cited reports with source attribution. Use when the user wants thorough research on any topic with evidence and citations.
- everything-claude-code:llm-trading-agent-security: Security patterns for autonomous trading agents with wallet or transaction authority. Covers prompt injection, spend limits, pre-send simulation, circuit breakers, MEV protection, and key handling.
- everything-claude-code:liquid-glass-design: iOS 26 Liquid Glass design system — dynamic glass material with blur, reflection, and interactive morphing for SwiftUI, UIKit, and WidgetKit.
- everything-claude-code:lead-intelligence: AI-native lead intelligence and outreach pipeline. Replaces Apollo, Clay, and ZoomInfo with agent-powered signal scoring, mutual ranking, warm path discovery, source-derived voice modeling, and channel-specific outreach across email, LinkedIn, and X. Use when the user wants to find, qualify, and reach high-value contacts.
- everything-claude-code:database-migrations: Database migration best practices for schema changes, data migrations, rollbacks, and zero-downtime deployments across PostgreSQL, MySQL, and common ORMs (Prisma, Drizzle, Kysely, Django, TypeORM, golang-migrate).
- everything-claude-code:data-scraper-agent: Build a fully automated AI-powered data collection agent for any public source — job boards, prices, news, GitHub, sports, anything. Scrapes on a schedule, enriches data with a free LLM (Gemini Flash), stores results in Notion/Sheets/Supabase, and learns from user feedback. Runs 100% free on GitHub Actions. Use when the user wants to monitor, collect, or track any public data automatically.
- everything-claude-code:laravel-verification: Verification loop for Laravel projects: env checks, linting, static analysis, tests with coverage, security scans, and deployment readiness.
- everything-claude-code:dashboard-builder: Build monitoring dashboards that answer real operator questions for Grafana, SigNoz, and similar platforms. Use when turning metrics into a working dashboard instead of a vanity board.
- everything-claude-code:laravel-tdd: Test-driven development for Laravel with PHPUnit and Pest, factories, database testing, fakes, and coverage targets.
- everything-claude-code:carrier-relationship-management: Codified expertise for managing carrier portfolios, negotiating freight rates, tracking carrier performance, allocating freight, and maintaining strategic carrier relationships. Informed by transportation managers with 15+ years experience. Includes scorecarding frameworks, RFP processes, market intelligence, and compliance vetting. Use when managing carriers, negotiating rates, evaluating carrier performance, or building freight strategies.
- everything-claude-code:customs-trade-compliance: Codified expertise for customs documentation, tariff classification, duty optimization, restricted party screening, and regulatory compliance across multiple jurisdictions. Informed by trade compliance specialists with 15+ years experience. Includes HS classification logic, Incoterms application, FTA utilization, and penalty mitigation. Use when handling customs clearance, tariff classification, trade compliance, import/export documentation, or duty optimization.
- everything-claude-code:customer-billing-ops: Operate customer billing workflows such as subscriptions, refunds, churn triage, billing-portal recovery, and plan analysis using connected billing tools like Stripe. Use when the user needs to help a customer, inspect subscription state, or manage revenue-impacting billing operations.

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review

content.content:
1	# TrmWordDict MCP Server 설계 (Next.js + Vercel)
2	
3	| 항목 | 값 |
4	|---|---|
5	| 작성일 | 2026-06-04 |
6	| 작성자 | Claude (brainstorming 결과) |
7	| 적용 범위 | `yg_v0.2` 브랜치 — Next.js 15 + Prisma + Supabase Cloud 스택 |
8	| 대체 대상 | `docs/plans/2026-05-20-mcp-server-design.md` (Spring Boot 기반 설계) — 폐기 |
9	
10	---
11	
12	## 0. 배경
13	
14	2026-05-29에 시스템 스택이 Spring Boot 2.7.18 → Next.js 15 + Prisma + Supabase로 전환됐다. 2026-05-20 작성된 SpringBoot 기반 MCP 설계는 미구현 상태에서 스택 불일치로 폐기 대상이 됐다.
15	
16	사용자 요구:
17	1. 특정 사이트/프로젝트에 제한되지 않고 **공용**으로 사용 가능해야 한다.
18	2. **MCP 서버**로서의 역할을 제공하여 사용자가 쉽게 사용할 수 있어야 한다.
19	3. 현 시스템에서 부족한 점을 **평가하고 업데이트**해야 한다.
20	
21	본 설계는 위 요구에 맞춰 신규 스택에 통합된 MCP 서버를 정의한다.
22	
23	---
24	
25	## 1. 목적
26	
27	TrmWordDict가 보유한 행안부 공통표준 단어/도메인/금칙어 데이터를 Claude Code · Cursor · 사내 Agent 등 MCP 클라이언트에 노출한다. 개발자는 자연어로 표준 준수 여부를 즉시 검증·제안받고, 비표준 용어 등록과 검수 큐 제안까지 자연스러운 워크플로우로 수행한다.
28	
29	---
30	
31	## 2. 설계 결정 요약
32	
33	| # | 축 | 결정 |
34	|:---:|---|---|
35	| 1 | 공용 범위 | 사내 공용 (단일 DB, 테넌트 분리 없음) |
36	| 2 | 구현 스택 | Next.js 15 App Router + `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` + Streamable HTTP |
37	| 3 | Tool 범위 | 전면 재평가 + 9개 (검색 4 / 검증 2 / 제안 1 / 쓰기 2) |
38	| 4 | 인증 | Personal Access Token (PAT) + SHA-256 해시 저장 |
39	| 5 | 배포 | Vercel + Supabase Cloud (HTTPS) |
40	| 6 | Rate Limit | Supabase Postgres 내부 (`TbTrmMcpRateLimit` + atomic upsert) |
41	| 7 | 출시 방식 | 설계는 전체 + 구현은 MVP부터 단계적 (Phase 1 → 2 → 3) |
42	
43	---
44	
45	## 3. 아키텍처 개요
46	
47	```
48	┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
49	│  MCP Client (Claude Code / Cursor / 사내 Agent)            │
50	│  ~/.claude/settings.json:                                  │
51	│  {                                                         │
52	│    "trmdict": {                                            │
53	│      "url": "https://trmdict.vercel.app/api/mcp",          │
54	│      "type": "http",                                       │
55	│      "headers": { "Authorization": "Bearer <PAT>" }        │
56	│    }                                                       │
57	│  }                                                         │
58	└────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────┘
59	                         │ Streamable HTTP (MCP 2025-03-26 spec)
60	                         │ JSON-RPC 2.0 over POST
61	                         ▼
62	┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
63	│  Vercel — Next.js 15 App Router                            │
64	│  app/api/mcp/route.ts    ← @modelcontextprotocol/sdk      │
65	│      │                                                     │
66	│      ├─ Auth Middleware (PAT 검증)                         │
67	│      ├─ Rate Limiter (Postgres atomic upsert)              │
68	│      ├─ MCP Server (tool registry)                         │
69	│      ├─ Tool Handlers (features/mcp/tools/*)               │
70	│      └─ Audit Log (fire-and-forget)                        │
71	└────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────┘
72	                         │ Prisma 6
73	                         ▼
74	┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
75	│  Supabase Cloud — PostgreSQL                               │
76	│  TbStdWord / TbStdDomain / TbTrmWord / TbTrmDomain /       │
77	│  TbTrmTerm / TbTrmForbiddenWord / TbTrmInspection /        │
78	│  TbTrmCustomWord / TbTrmMcpToken / TbTrmMcpRateLimit /     │
79	│  TbTrmMcpAuditLog                                          │
80	└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
81	```
82	
83	### 3.1 핵심 결정
84	
85	| 항목 | 결정 |
86	|---|---|
87	| MCP SDK | `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` (공식 TypeScript SDK) |
88	| Transport | Streamable HTTP (2025-03-26 spec) — Vercel Serverless 친화 |
89	| 엔드포인트 | `POST /api/mcp` (단일) — JSON-RPC 2.0 |
90	| 인증 | `Authorization: Bearer <PAT>` 헤더 |
91	| 세션 모델 | Stateless (요청 독립, 세션 캐시 불필요) |
92	| 패키지 위치 | `features/mcp/` (기존 패턴 따름) + `app/api/mcp/route.ts` |
93	
94	### 3.2 기존 설계(2026-05-20)와의 차이
95	
96	| 측면 | 기존 (폐기) | 본 설계 |
97	|---|---|---|
98	| 백엔드 | Spring Boot 2.7.18 + Tibero | Next.js 15 + Supabase |
99	| SDK | (없음) 수동 구현 | `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` 공식 |
100	| Transport | SSE 2024-11-05 | Streamable HTTP 2025-03-26 |
101	| 세션 | Stateful (`ConcurrentHashMap<SseEmitter>`) | Stateless |
102	| 인증 | `permitAll` 내부망 | PAT (`Authorization: Bearer`) |
103	| Tool 수 | 6개 | 9개 (도메인 확장) |
104	| 배포 | 내부망 단일 인스턴스 | Vercel Edge/Serverless |
105

107	
108	## 4. 공용성(여러 도메인/프로젝트 동시 사용) 설계
109	
110	### 4.1 공용 사용 시나리오
111	
112	```
113	┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────┐
114	│  프로젝트 A     │  │  프로젝트 B     │  │  사내 신규Agent │
115	│ (Claude Code)   │  │  (Cursor)       │  │  (custom MCP)   │
116	│ PAT: pat_a...   │  │  PAT: pat_b...  │  │  PAT: pat_c...  │
117	└────────┬────────┘  └────────┬────────┘  └────────┬────────┘
118	         └────────────────────┴────────────────────┘
119	                              │ 같은 URL
120	                              ▼
121	                  https://trmdict.vercel.app/api/mcp
122	```
123	
124	### 4.2 공용성 보장 5가지 메커니즘
125	
126	| # | 메커니즘 | 효과 |
127	|:---:|---|---|
128	| 1 | **단일 공용 URL** | `POST /api/mcp` 하나. 어떤 프로젝트든 동일 엔드포인트. 신규 프로젝트 합류 시 서버 변경 없음 |
129	| 2 | **Stateless HTTP** | 세션 저장 없음 → 동시 다중 사용자 무제한 확장. connection limit 없음 |
130	| 3 | **사용자별 PAT** | 각 개발자가 자기 PAT 발급. PAT 이름으로 식별 ("회사 PC", "노트북") |
131	| 4 | **데이터 공유 + 사용자 식별 분리** | 표준 데이터는 read-only 공용. 등록/제안에는 `user_id`/`rgtrId` 기록 |
132	| 5 | **표준 MCP Spec 준수** | Claude Code/Cursor/사내 Agent 모두 동일 spec → 추가 어댑터 불필요 |
133	
134	### 4.3 합류 절차 (신규 사용자)
135	
136	1. Supabase Auth 가입 (초대 또는 직접 가입)
137	2. `/admin/settings` 또는 `/app/settings`에서 "MCP PAT 발급" 클릭
138	3. 발급된 토큰을 IDE/Agent `settings.json`에 등록
139	4. 끝 — 서버 변경 / 화이트리스트 / 별도 등록 절차 없음
140	
141	### 4.4 명시적으로 *공용 아닌* 부분
142	
143	- 테넌트 분리 (회사 A vs 회사 B 데이터 격리) — SaaS 모델로 향후 Phase 3 결정 시
144	- 프로젝트별 권한 격리 — 사내라 모두 동일 데이터 풀
145	- 다국어 표준 — 행안부 한국어 표준만 (영문 약어 매칭은 지원)
146

150	
151	### 5.1 검색/조회 Tools (USER 권한)
152	
153	#### 5.1.1 `search_term` — 통합 검색

156	|---|---|
157	| 권한 | USER |
158	| 입력 | `{ query: string, type: "all"\|"word"\|"domain"\|"term"\|"forbidden", limit?: number }` |
159	| 출력 | `{ results: Array<{ type, id, name, abbr?, ... }> }` |
160	
161	#### 5.1.2 `get_word_detail` — 표준 단어 상세
162	
163	| 항목 | 값 |
164	|---|---|
165	| 권한 | USER |
166	| 입력 | `{ abbr: string }` 또는 `{ id: number }` |
167	| 출력 | `{ stdWordNm, engAbbrNm, engNm, domain, ... }` |
168	
169	#### 5.1.3 `get_domain_detail` — 표준 도메인 상세
170	
171	| 항목 | 값 |
172	|---|---|
173	| 권한 | USER |
174	| 입력 | `{ name: string }` |
175	| 출력 | `{ name, dataType, description, logicalType }` |
176	
177	#### 5.1.4 `list_forbidden_words` — 금칙어 목록
178	
179	| 항목 | 값 |
180	|---|---|
181	| 권한 | USER |
182	| 입력 | `{ category?: "all"\|"naming"\|"content", limit?: number }` |
183	| 출력 | `{ items: Array<{ word, reason }> }` |
184	
185	### 5.2 검증 Tools (USER 권한)
186	
187	#### 5.2.1 `validate_identifier` — DB/Java 식별자 통합 검증
188	
189	| 항목 | 값 |
190	|---|---|
191	| 권한 | USER |
192	| 입력 | `{ name: string, kind: "db_column"\|"db_table"\|"java_class"\|"java_field"\|"java_method" }` |
193	| 출력 | `{ valid, tokens, invalidTokens, suggestionMessage? }` |
194	
195	검증 로직:
196	1. `kind`에 따라 토큰 분리 (DB는 `_`, Java는 camelCase)
197	2. 각 토큰을 `TbStdWord.engAbbrNm` 우선 조회 → 매칭 시 `source: "standard"`
198	3. 미매칭 토큰은 `TbTrmCustomWord.wordEngAbbrNm` 2차 조회 → `source: "custom"`
199	4. 전부 매칭이면 `valid: true`, 일부 누락이면 `suggestionMessage`로 등록 안내
200	
201	#### 5.2.2 `check_forbidden_in_text` — 텍스트 일괄 금칙어 스캔
202	
203	| 항목 | 값 |
204	|---|---|
205	| 권한 | USER |
206	| 입력 | `{ text: string }` |
207	| 출력 | `{ hasForbidden, matches: Array<{ word, position, reason }> }` |
208	
209	### 5.3 제안 Tools (USER 권한)
210	
211	#### 5.3.1 `suggest_naming` — 한글 → 식별자 제안
212	
213	| 항목 | 값 |
214	|---|---|
215	| 권한 | USER |
216	| 입력 | `{ koreanDescription: string, target: "db_column"\|"java_field"\|"both" }` |
217	| 출력 | `{ dbColumn?, javaField?, confidence: "high"\|"medium"\|"low", basedOn }` |
218	
219	### 5.4 쓰기 Tools
220	
221	#### 5.4.1 `register_custom_word` — 비표준 용어 등록
222	
223	| 항목 | 값 |
224	|---|---|
225	| 권한 | **ADMIN** |
226	| 입력 | `{ koreanName, englishName?, englishAbbr?, reason? }` |
227	| 출력 | `{ id, status: "created" }` |
228	
229	#### 5.4.2 `propose_term` — 신규 용어 제안 (검수 큐 등록)
230	
231	| 항목 | 값 |
232	|---|---|
233	| 권한 | USER (등록은 검수 큐), ADMIN (즉시 승인 옵션) |
234	| 입력 | `{ type: "word"\|"domain"\|"term", payload: object, reason: string }` |
235	| 출력 | `{ inspectionId, status: "pending_review" }` |
236	
237	기존 Inspection 모듈(`TbTrmInspection`)에 PENDING 상태로 INSERT.
238	
239	### 5.5 기존 6개 vs 신규 9개 매핑
240	
241	| 기존 (2026-05-20 설계) | 신규 | 변화 |
242	|---|---|---|
243	| search_standard_word | search_term | 통합 검색으로 확장 |
244	| validate_db_name | validate_identifier | java_identifier와 통합 |
245	| validate_java_identifier | (위와 통합) | |
246	| suggest_db_column_name | suggest_naming | java_field 동시 제안 |
247	| create_custom_word | register_custom_word | 이름 정리 |
248	| search_custom_word | search_term의 custom 옵션 | 통합 검색에 흡수 |
249	| (없음) | get_word_detail / get_domain_detail / list_forbidden_words | 신규 조회 |
250	| (없음) | check_forbidden_in_text | 신규 검증 |
251	| (없음) | propose_term | 검수 큐 연동 |

254	
255	## 6. 인증 / 권한
256	
257	### 6.1 Personal Access Token (PAT) 모델
258	
259	Supabase JWT는 만료가 짧아(1시간) MCP 클라이언트에 부적합. 별도 PAT 발급 시스템 운영.
260	
261	```
262	1. 사용자 웹 로그인 (Supabase Auth)
263	        ↓
264	2. /admin/settings (또는 /app/settings) → "MCP Personal Access Token" 발급
265	        ↓
266	3. PAT 생성: trmdict_pat_{32바이트 랜덤}
267	   DB: TbTrmMcpToken (token_hash SHA-256, user_id, name, expires_at, last_used_at, revoked_at)
268	        ↓
269	4. 일회성 표시 (다시 못 봄) → 사용자가 복사
270	        ↓
271	5. ~/.claude/settings.json에 Bearer로 등록
272	```
273	
274	### 6.2 서버 측 검증 흐름
275	
276	```
277	POST /api/mcp
278	        ↓
279	1. Authorization 헤더 추출 → "trmdict_pat_..." 검증
280	2. SHA-256 해시 → TbTrmMcpToken.token_hash 조회
281	3. revoked_at IS NULL && (expires_at IS NULL OR expires_at > now())
282	4. AuthUser 조회 → role (USER/ADMIN) 확인
283	5. Tool 권한 체크 → 실행 또는 -32004 권한 거부
284	6. TbTrmMcpToken.last_used_at = now() (비동기)
285	```
286	
287	### 6.3 권한 매트릭스
288	
289	| # | Tool | USER | ADMIN |
290	|:---:|---|:---:|:---:|
291	| 1 | search_term | ✓ | ✓ |
292	| 2 | get_word_detail | ✓ | ✓ |
293	| 3 | get_domain_detail | ✓ | ✓ |
294	| 4 | list_forbidden_words | ✓ | ✓ |
295	| 5 | validate_identifier | ✓ | ✓ |
296	| 6 | check_forbidden_in_text | ✓ | ✓ |
297	| 7 | suggest_naming | ✓ | ✓ |
298	| 8 | register_custom_word | ✗ | ✓ |
299	| 9 | propose_term | ✓ (검수 큐) | ✓ (즉시 승인 가능) |
300	
301	### 6.4 Rate Limit
302	
303	| 권한 | 분당 한도 | 일일 한도 |
304	|---|---:|---:|
305	| USER | 60 req/min | 5,000 req/day |
306	| ADMIN | 200 req/min | 20,000 req/day |
307	| 미인증 | 거부 (-32001) | — |
308	
309	키 구조: `(token_id, window_key)` — `window_key`는 분 단위 `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MI` 또는 일 단위 `YYYY-MM-DD`.
310	
311	### 6.5 보안 추가 결정
312	
313	| 항목 | 결정 |
314	|---|---|
315	| HTTPS | Vercel 자동 (필수) |
316	| CORS | 명시적 origin만 (와일드카드 금지) |
317	| PAT 회전 | UI에서 명시적 revoke 또는 `expires_at` 설정 |
318	| 감사 로그 | `TbTrmMcpAuditLog` — 모든 Tool 호출 기록 |
319	| 토큰 평문 저장 | 절대 금지 — SHA-256 해시만 |
320	
321	---
322	
323	## 7. 데이터 모델
324	
325	### 7.1 신규 테이블 4개 (Prisma schema)
326	
327	```prisma
328	model TbTrmMcpToken {
329	  id          BigInt    @id @default(autoincrement())
330	  tokenHash   String    @unique @map("token_hash")
331	  name        String
332	  userId      String    @map("user_id")
333	  scopes      String[]  @default(["read", "write"])
334	  expiresAt   DateTime? @map("expires_at")
335	  lastUsedAt  DateTime? @map("last_used_at")
336	  revokedAt   DateTime? @map("revoked_at")
337	  createdAt   DateTime  @default(now()) @map("created_at")
338	
339	  user        AuthUser  @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id])
340	  rateLimits  TbTrmMcpRateLimit[]
341	  auditLogs   TbTrmMcpAuditLog[]
342	
343	  @@index([userId])
344	  @@map("tb_trm_mcp_token")
345	}
346	
347	model TbTrmMcpRateLimit {
348	  tokenId    BigInt   @map("token_id")
349	  windowKey  String   @map("window_key")
350	  count      Int      @default(0)
351	
352	  token      TbTrmMcpToken @relation(fields: [tokenId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
353	
354	  @@id([tokenId, windowKey])
355	  @@map("tb_trm_mcp_rate_limit")
356	}
357	
358	model TbTrmMcpAuditLog {
359	  id          BigInt   @id @default(autoincrement())
360	  tokenId     BigInt?  @map("token_id")
361	  userId      String?  @map("user_id")
362	  toolName    String   @map("tool_name")
363	  paramsHash  String?  @map("params_hash")
364	  status      String
365	  errorCode   Int?     @map("error_code")
366	  latencyMs   Int?     @map("latency_ms")
367	  ts          DateTime @default(now())
368	
369	  token       TbTrmMcpToken? @relation(fields: [tokenId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)
370	
371	  @@index([userId, ts])
372	  @@index([toolName, ts])
373	  @@map("tb_trm_mcp_audit_log")
374	}
375	
376	model TbTrmCustomWord {
377	  id            BigInt    @id @default(autoincrement())
378	  wordKrNm      String    @map("word_kr_nm")
379	  wordEngNm     String?   @map("word_eng_nm")
380	  wordEngAbbrNm String?   @map("word_eng_abbr_nm")
381	  reasonCn      String?   @map("reason_cn")
382	  useYn         String    @default("Y") @map("use_yn")
383	  rgtrId        String?   @map("rgtr_id")
384	  regDt         DateTime  @default(now()) @map("reg_dt")
385	  mdfrId        String?   @map("mdfr_id")
386	  mdfcnDt       DateTime? @updatedAt @map("mdfcn_dt")
387	
388	  @@index([wordEngAbbrNm])
389	  @@index([wordKrNm])
390	  @@map("tb_trm_custom_word")
391	}
392	```
393	
394	### 7.2 기존 테이블 영향
395	
396	| 테이블 | 영향 | 비고 |
397	|---|---|---|
398	| `TbStdWord` (행안부 표준 단어) | 읽기 only | 변경 없음 |
399	| `TbStdDomain` | 읽기 only | 변경 없음 |
400	| `TbTrmWord/Domain/Term` | 읽기 only | 변경 없음 |
401	| `TbTrmForbiddenWord` | 읽기 only | 변경 없음 |
402	| `TbTrmInspection` (yg_v0.2 기존) | **쓰기** | propose_term이 PENDING 상태로 INSERT |
403	| `AuthUser` (Supabase auth.users) | 읽기 only | PAT가 FK로 참조 |
404	
405	### 7.3 데이터 시드 평가 (현 시스템 평가)
406	
407	| 항목 | 현재 상태 | 필요 조치 |
408	|---|---|---|
409	| TbStdWord (행안부 표준 단어 약 30K행) | ❓ 미확인 — Tibero → Supabase 마이그레이션 여부 불명 | MVP 시작 전 검증 필수 |
410	| TbStdDomain | ❓ 동일 | 동일 |
411	| TbTrmCustomWord | 신규 | 빈 상태로 시작 |
412	| TbTrmForbiddenWord | ❓ Inspection UI 작동 가정 | DB 데이터 존재 확인 |
413	
414	### 7.4 Migration / Cleanup
415	
416	```bash
417	# 스키마 변경 → migration 파일 생성
418	pnpm prisma migrate dev --name add_mcp_tables
419	
420	# 운영(Vercel + Supabase Cloud) 적용
421	pnpm prisma migrate deploy
422	```
423	
424	```sql
425	-- pg_cron 일일 cleanup (Supabase Dashboard 등록)
426	SELECT cron.schedule('mcp-ratelimit-cleanup', '0 0 * * *',
427	  $$DELETE FROM tb_trm_mcp_rate_limit
428	    WHERE window_key < to_char(now() - interval '2 days', 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI')$$);
429	
430	SELECT cron.schedule('mcp-auditlog-cleanup', '0 1 * * *',
431	  $$DELETE FROM tb_trm_mcp_audit_log WHERE ts < now() - interval '90 days'$$);
432	```
433	
434	---
435	
436	## 8. 에러 처리 / JSON-RPC 응답 규격
437	
438	### 8.1 에러 코드
439	
440	| Code | 의미 | 발생 시점 | HTTP |
441	|---:|---|---|:---:|
442	| -32700 | Parse error | JSON 파싱 실패 | 400 |
443	| -32600 | Invalid Request | jsonrpc/method 누락 | 400 |
444	| -32601 | Method not found | 존재하지 않는 method | 200 |
445	| -32602 | Invalid params | Tool 입력 스키마 검증 실패 | 200 |
446	| -32603 | Internal error | DB 오류, 예외 | 200 |
447	| **-32001** | Authentication required | Authorization 헤더 없음 | **401** |
448	| **-32002** | Invalid token | PAT 해시 미매칭/만료/revoked | **401** |
449	| **-32003** | Rate limit exceeded | 한도 초과 | **429** |
450	| **-32004** | Permission denied | USER가 ADMIN Tool 호출 | **403** |
451	| **-32005** | Tool not found | 존재하지 않는 tool name | 200 |
452	
453	> **-32001 ~ -32005**: MCP 표준 외 본 서버 확장 코드
454	
455	### 8.2 응답 규격
456	
457	성공:
458	```json
459	{
460	  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
461	  "id": 7,
462	  "result": {
463	    "content": [{ "type": "text", "text": "{...}" }]
464	  }
465	}
466	```
467	
468	Tool 내부 에러 (`isError`):
469	```json
470	{
471	  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
472	  "id": 7,
473	  "result": {
474	    "content": [{ "type": "text", "text": "검색어가 비어있습니다." }],
475	    "isError": true
476	  }
477	}
478	```
479	
480	프로토콜 에러:
481	```json
482	{
483	  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
484	  "id": 7,
485	  "error": {
486	    "code": -32003,
487	    "message": "Rate limit exceeded",
488	    "data": { "limit": 60, "windowSeconds": 60, "retryAfter": 23 }
489	  }
490	}
491	```
492	
493	### 8.3 감사 로그 기록 정책
494	
495	| 시점 | 기록 | 비고 |
496	|---|:---:|---|
497	| 모든 Tool 호출 | ✓ | success/error/denied 모두 |
498	| 인증 실패 (-32001/-32002) | ✓ | token_id/user_id NULL, status="denied" |
499	| 권한 거부 (-32004) | ✓ | status="denied" |
500	| Rate Limit (-32003) | ✗ | 카운트만, 별도 로그 X |
501	| 파싱 에러 (-32700) | ✗ | 무효 요청 부담 회피 |
502	
503	- 비동기 (fire-and-forget)
504	- `params_hash`: SHA-256 of arguments JSON (민감 정보 미저장)
505	- 보존: 90일
506	
507	---
508	
509	## 9. 테스트 전략
510	
511	### 9.1 테스트 피라미드
512	
513	| 레이어 | 도구 | 수량 |
514	|---|---|---:|
515	| ① Tool Handler 단위 (Mock Prisma) | Vitest | ~30건 |
516	| ② Service/Repository 단위 | Vitest + vitest-mock-extended | ~20건 |
517	| ③ 통합 (Route + DB) | Vitest + next-test-api-route-handler | 12건 |
518	| ④ 수동 E2E (Claude Code 연결) | 수동 | 3건 |
519	
520	### 9.2 통합 테스트 12건 (필수 시나리오)
521	
522	| # | 시나리오 | 기대 |
523	|:---:|---|---|
524	| 1 | Auth 헤더 없음 | -32001 + HTTP 401 |
525	| 2 | 무효 PAT | -32002 + HTTP 401 |
526	| 3 | revoked PAT | -32002 + HTTP 401 |
527	| 4 | 만료 PAT | -32002 + HTTP 401 |
528	| 5 | `initialize` | capabilities |
529	| 6 | `tools/list` | tools 9개 |
530	| 7 | `tools/call: search_term` (USER) | results 배열 |
531	| 8 | `register_custom_word` (USER) | -32004 |
532	| 9 | `register_custom_word` (ADMIN) | created |
533	| 10 | Rate limit 초과 (61회) | -32003 + 429 + Retry-After |
534	| 11 | 잘못된 method | -32601 |
535	| 12 | 입력 스키마 위반 | -32602 + data.errors |
536	
537	### 9.3 커버리지 목표
538	
539	| 항목 | 목표 |
540	|---|---|
541	| Tool handler 분기 커버리지 | ≥ 90% |
542	| Auth/RateLimit 분기 커버리지 | **100%** |
543	| 통합 테스트 통과율 | 12/12 |
544	| CI 테스트 시간 | ≤ 60초 |
545	
546	---
547	
548	## 10. 출시 단계 (MVP → Phase 2 → Phase 3)
549	
550	### 10.1 Phase 1 (MVP) — 약 2주 · Task 10개
551	
552	**목표:** Claude Code에서 자연어 *"이 컬럼명 표준이야?"* 질의로 자동 검증 워크플로우 작동
553	
554	| # | 구성 요소 |
555	|:---:|---|
556	| 1 | DB 마이그레이션 — 신규 4 테이블 |
557	| 2 | 데이터 시드 검증 — TbStdWord/Domain 존재 확인 |
558	| 3 | PAT 발급 UI — `/admin/settings`에 섹션 추가 |
559	| 4 | 인증 미들웨어 — `verifyPat()` |
560	| 5 | Rate Limit 카운터 — atomic upsert |
561	| 6 | 감사 로그 — fire-and-forget 기록 |
562	| 7 | MCP Server 인프라 — SDK + tool registry + route.ts |
563	| 8 | 핵심 Tool 5개 — search_term / validate_identifier / suggest_naming / get_word_detail / check_forbidden_in_text |
564	| 9 | 통합 테스트 12건 |
565	| 10 | Vercel 배포 + Claude Code E2E |
566	
567	### 10.2 Phase 2 — 약 1주 · Task 6개
568	
569	| # | 구성 요소 |
570	|:---:|---|
571	| 1 | Tool 4개 추가 — get_domain_detail / list_forbidden_words / register_custom_word / propose_term |
572	| 2 | Inspection 모듈 연동 — propose_term → TbTrmInspection |
573	| 3 | PAT 관리 화면 강화 — 사용 통계, 마지막 사용 시각 |
574	| 4 | MCP 호출 통계 대시보드 — `/admin/stats` |
575	| 5 | 에러율 알람 — Vercel Integration → 슬랙 |
576	| 6 | pg_cron cleanup 정착 |
577	
578	### 10.3 Phase 3 (선택적)
579	
580	| 후보 | 트리거 |
581	|---|---|
582	| 다른 MCP 클라이언트 호환 검증 | 사내 도입 요청 |
583	| 추가 Tool (schema diff, fuzzy search) | 사용 패턴 분석 |
584	| 다국어/타 기관 표준 지원 | 비즈니스 요구 |
585	| Tenant 분리 (다회사 SaaS) | "사내 공용" 정책 변경 |
586	
587	### 10.4 Definition of Done
588	
589	**Phase 1 DoD:**
590	- [ ] Vercel production 배포 완료, HTTPS URL 유효
591	- [ ] Claude Code에서 `/mcp` → trmdict 5 tool 노출
592	- [ ] "USER_NM 이 표준이야?" 자연어 → validate_identifier 자동 호출, valid:true
593	- [ ] "BLKCHAIN_ID 검증" → invalid + 제안 응답
594	- [ ] 통합 테스트 12/12 PASS, 단위 테스트 ≥ 90% 커버리지
595	- [ ] 다른 팀원 1명이 자기 PAT 발급 후 동일 검증 (공용 동작 확인)
596	
597	**Phase 2 DoD:**
598	- [ ] 9개 Tool 전체 동작
599	- [ ] Inspection 검수 큐에 MCP 경유 propose_term 적재
600	- [ ] `/admin/stats`에 MCP 통계 시각화
601	
602	### 10.5 리스크 관리
603	
604	| 리스크 | Phase 1 대응 |
605	|---|---|
606	| Supabase에 표준 데이터 없음 | 시작 전 검증. 없으면 Tibero → Supabase 마이그레이션 또는 import job 선행 |
607	| `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` Streamable HTTP 미성숙 | 출시 확인. 미흡 시 SSE fallback |
608	| PAT 평문 유출 | UI 일회성 표시 + 즉시 revoke + 감사 로그 추적 |
609	| 사내 다른 시스템과 충돌 | 별도 도메인(`trmdict.vercel.app`) 사용 |
610	
611	---
612	
613	## 11. Claude Code 연결 설정 예시
614	
615	```json
616	{
617	  "mcpServers": {
618	    "trmdict": {
619	      "url": "https://trmdict.vercel.app/api/mcp",
620	      "type": "http",
621	      "headers": {
622	        "Authorization": "Bearer trmdict_pat_a1b2c3..."
623	      }
624	    }
625	  }
626	}
627	```
628	
629	확인: Claude Code에서 `/mcp` 명령으로 trmdict 서버가 연결되는지 + 9개 tool 메타데이터 확인.
630	
631	---
632	
633	## 12. 후속 작업
634	
635	본 설계 승인 후 `superpowers:writing-plans` 스킬로 Phase 1 (MVP) Task 10개의 상세 구현 계획서를 작성한다. 산출물 위치: `docs/plans/2026-06-04-mcp-server-plan.md`.
636	
637	각 Task는 TDD 5단계를 강제:
638	1. 실패 테스트 작성
639	2. 실패 확인
640	3. 구현
641	4. 통과 확인
642	5. 커밋

1	export const MCP_ERROR = {
2	  PARSE_ERROR: -32700,
3	  INVALID_REQUEST: -32600,
4	  METHOD_NOT_FOUND: -32601,
5	  INVALID_PARAMS: -32602,
6	  INTERNAL_ERROR: -32603,
7	  AUTH_REQUIRED: -32001,
8	  INVALID_TOKEN: -32002,
9	  RATE_LIMIT: -32003,
10	  PERMISSION_DENIED: -32004,
11	  TOOL_NOT_FOUND: -32005,
12	} as const
13	
14	export type McpErrorCode = (typeof MCP_ERROR)[keyof typeof MCP_ERROR]
15	export type AuthContext = { tokenId: bigint; userId: string; role: 'USER' | 'ADMIN' }
16	export type AuthResult =
17	  | { ok: true; tokenId: bigint; userId: string; role: 'USER' | 'ADMIN' }
18	  | { ok: false; errorCode: McpErrorCode; message: string }
19

1	import { createHash } from 'crypto'
2	import { prisma } from '@/lib/prisma'
3	
4	export type AuditInput = {
5	  tokenId: bigint | null
6	  userId: string | null
7	  toolName: string
8	  params: unknown
9	  status: 'success' | 'error' | 'denied'
10	  errorCode?: number
11	  latencyMs?: number
12	}
13	
14	export async function logAudit(input: AuditInput): Promise<void> {
15	  try {
16	    const paramsHash = createHash('sha256')
17	      .update(JSON.stringify(input.params ?? {}))
18	      .digest('hex')
19	    await prisma.tbTrmMcpAuditLog.create({
20	      data: {
21	        tokenId: input.tokenId ?? undefined,
22	        userId: input.userId ?? undefined,
23	        toolName: input.toolName,
24	        paramsHash,
25	        status: input.status,
26	        errorCode: input.errorCode,
27	        latencyMs: input.latencyMs,
28	      },
29	    })
30	  } catch (e) {
31	    // fire-and-forget: never throw
32	    console.error('[mcp-audit] failed:', e)
33	  }
34	}
35

1	import { prisma } from '@/lib/prisma'
2	import { requireAuth } from '@/lib/auth'
3	import type { McpTokenListItem } from './types'
4	
5	export async function listMyTokens(): Promise<McpTokenListItem[]> {
6	  const session = await requireAuth()
7	  return prisma.tbTrmMcpToken.findMany({
8	    where: { userId: session.user.id },
9	    select: { id: true, name: true, createdAt: true, lastUsedAt: true, expiresAt: true, revokedAt: true },
10	    orderBy: { createdAt: 'desc' },
11	  })
12	}
13

1	'use server'
2	import { randomBytes, createHash } from 'crypto'
3	import { prisma } from '@/lib/prisma'
4	import { requireAuth } from '@/lib/auth'
5	import { revalidatePath } from 'next/cache'
6	import type { IssueTokenInput, IssueTokenResult } from './types'
7	
8	export async function issueToken(input: IssueTokenInput): Promise<IssueTokenResult> {
9	  const session = await requireAuth()
10	  const raw = randomBytes(32).toString('hex')
11	  const token = `trmdict_pat_${raw}`
12	  const tokenHash = createHash('sha256').update(token).digest('hex')
13	  const expiresAt = input.expiresInDays
14	    ? new Date(Date.now() + input.expiresInDays * 86_400_000)
15	    : null
16	  const created = await prisma.tbTrmMcpToken.create({
17	    data: { tokenHash, name: input.name, userId: session.user.id, expiresAt },
18	  })
19	  revalidatePath('/admin/settings')
20	  return { id: created.id, token }
21	}
22	
23	export async function revokeToken(id: bigint): Promise<void> {
24	  const session = await requireAuth()
25	  const owned = await prisma.tbTrmMcpToken.findFirst({ where: { id, userId: session.user.id } })
26	  if (!owned) throw new Error('Token not found')
27	  await prisma.tbTrmMcpToken.update({ where: { id }, data: { revokedAt: new Date() } })
28	  revalidatePath('/admin/settings')
29	}

1	import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest'
2	import { logAudit } from '../log'
3	import { prismaMock } from '@/vitest.setup'
4	
5	describe('logAudit', () => {
6	  it('inserts audit record with SHA-256 params hash', async () => {
7	    prismaMock.tbTrmMcpAuditLog.create.mockResolvedValue({} as any)
8	    await logAudit({
9	      tokenId: 1n, userId: 'u', toolName: 'search_term',
10	      params: { query: '사용자' }, status: 'success', latencyMs: 45,
11	    })
12	    expect(prismaMock.tbTrmMcpAuditLog.create).toHaveBeenCalledWith({
13	      data: expect.objectContaining({
14	        tokenId: 1n,
15	        userId: 'u',
16	        toolName: 'search_term',
17	        paramsHash: expect.stringMatching(/^[a-f0-9]{64}$/),
18	        status: 'success',
19	        latencyMs: 45,
20	      }),
21	    })
22	  })
23	
24	  it('does not throw when DB insert fails (fire-and-forget)', async () => {
25	    prismaMock.tbTrmMcpAuditLog.create.mockRejectedValue(new Error('DB down'))
26	    await expect(logAudit({
27	      tokenId: 1n, userId: 'u', toolName: 'x', params: {}, status: 'success',
28	    })).resolves.not.toThrow()
29	  })
30	
31	  it('handles null tokenId/userId for unauthenticated requests', async () => {
32	    prismaMock.tbTrmMcpAuditLog.create.mockResolvedValue({} as any)
33	    await logAudit({
34	      tokenId: null, userId: null, toolName: '<auth-fail>',
35	      params: {}, status: 'denied', errorCode: -32001,
36	    })
37	    expect(prismaMock.tbTrmMcpAuditLog.create).toHaveBeenCalledWith({
38	      data: expect.objectContaining({
39	        status: 'denied',
40	        errorCode: -32001,
41	      }),
42	    })
43	  })
44	
45	  it('stores different hashes for different params', async () => {
46	    prismaMock.tbTrmMcpAuditLog.create.mockResolvedValue({} as any)
47	    await logAudit({ tokenId: 1n, userId: 'u', toolName: 'a', params: { x: 1 }, status: 'success' })
48	    const call1 = prismaMock.tbTrmMcpAuditLog.create.mock.calls[0][0].data.paramsHash
49	
50	    prismaMock.tbTrmMcpAuditLog.create.mockResolvedValue({} as any)
51	    await logAudit({ tokenId: 1n, userId: 'u', toolName: 'a', params: { x: 2 }, status: 'success' })
52	    const call2 = prismaMock.tbTrmMcpAuditLog.create.mock.calls[1][0].data.paramsHash
53	
54	    expect(call1).not.toBe(call2)
55	  })
56	})

1	import { describe, it, expect, vi } from 'vitest'
2	import { issueToken, revokeToken } from '../actions'
3	import { prismaMock } from '@/vitest.setup'
4	
5	vi.mock('@/lib/auth', () => ({
6	  requireAuth: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({ user: { id: 'user-uuid-1' }, access_token: 'mock' }),
7	}))
8	vi.mock('next/cache', () => ({ revalidatePath: vi.fn() }))

10	describe('issueToken', () => {
11	  it('generates trmdict_pat_ prefixed token and stores SHA-256 hash', async () => {
12	    prismaMock.tbTrmMcpToken.create.mockResolvedValue({ id: 1n } as any)
13	    const result = await issueToken({ name: 'my-laptop' })

15	    expect(prismaMock.tbTrmMcpToken.create).toHaveBeenCalledWith({

17	        tokenHash: expect.stringMatching(/^[a-f0-9]{64}$/),
18	        userId: 'user-uuid-1',
19	        name: 'my-laptop',
20	      }),
21	    })
22	  })
23	})
24	
25	describe('revokeToken', () => {
26	  it('sets revokedAt to now() for owned token', async () => {
27	    prismaMock.tbTrmMcpToken.findFirst.mockResolvedValue({ id: 5n, userId: 'user-uuid-1' } as any)
28	    prismaMock.tbTrmMcpToken.update.mockResolvedValue({} as any)
29	    await revokeToken(5n)
30	    expect(prismaMock.tbTrmMcpToken.update).toHaveBeenCalledWith({
31	      where: { id: 5n },
32	      data: { revokedAt: expect.any(Date) },
33	    })
34	  })
35	
36	  it('throws when token not owned by current user', async () => {
37	    prismaMock.tbTrmMcpToken.findFirst.mockResolvedValue(null)

39	  })

1	import { prisma } from '@/lib/prisma'
2	
3	export type SeedCheckResult = {
4	  ok: boolean
5	  counts: { stdWord: number; stdDomain: number; forbiddenWord: number }
6	  missingTables: string[]
7	}
8	
9	export async function checkSeed(): Promise<SeedCheckResult> {
10	  const [stdWord, stdDomain, forbiddenWord] = await Promise.all([
11	    prisma.word.count(),
12	    prisma.domain.count(),
13	    prisma.forbiddenWord.count(),
14	  ])
15	  const missingTables: string[] = []
16	  if (stdWord === 0) missingTables.push('TbStdWord')
17	  if (stdDomain === 0) missingTables.push('TbStdDomain')
18	  if (forbiddenWord === 0) missingTables.push('TbTrmForbiddenWord')
19	  return { ok: missingTables.length === 0, counts: { stdWord, stdDomain, forbiddenWord }, missingTables }
20	}
21

1	import { createSupabaseServerClient } from './supabase/server'
2	import { redirect } from 'next/navigation'
3	
4	export async function getSession() {
5	  const supabase = await createSupabaseServerClient()
6	  const { data: { session } } = await supabase.auth.getSession()
7	  return session
8	}
9	
10	export async function requireAuth() {
11	  const session = await getSession()
12	  if (!session) redirect('/login')
13	  return session
14	}
15	
16	export async function requireAdmin() {
17	  const session = await requireAuth()
18	  if (session.user.app_metadata?.role !== 'ADMIN') redirect('/403')
19	  return session
20	}
21

1	import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest'

4	
5	describe('checkSeed', () => {
6	  it('returns ok=true when all tables have rows', async () => {
7	    prismaMock.word.count.mockResolvedValue(30000)
8	    prismaMock.domain.count.mockResolvedValue(500)
9	    prismaMock.forbiddenWord.count.mockResolvedValue(50)
10	    const result = await checkSeed()
11	    expect(result.ok).toBe(true)
12	    expect(result.counts.stdWord).toBe(30000)
13	  })
14	
15	  it('returns ok=false and lists missing tables when empty', async () => {
16	    prismaMock.word.count.mockResolvedValue(0)
17	    prismaMock.domain.count.mockResolvedValue(0)
18	    prismaMock.forbiddenWord.count.mockResolvedValue(0)
19	    const result = await checkSeed()
20	    expect(result.ok).toBe(false)
21	    expect(result.missingTables).toContain('TbStdWord')
22	    expect(result.missingTables).toContain('TbStdDomain')
23	  })
24	
25	  it('ok=false only for empty tables (partial)', async () => {
26	    prismaMock.word.count.mockResolvedValue(100)
27	    prismaMock.domain.count.mockResolvedValue(0)
28	    prismaMock.forbiddenWord.count.mockResolvedValue(10)
29	    const result = await checkSeed()
30	    expect(result.ok).toBe(false)
31	    expect(result.missingTables).toEqual(['TbStdDomain'])
32	  })
33	})

content.text:
Now I have a comprehensive picture of the current state. Here is my summary and the implementation plan.

---

## Current State Summary

**Already implemented (Phase 1 partial):**
- Prisma schema: all 4 MCP tables added (`TbTrmMcpToken`, `TbTrmMcpRateLimit`, `TbTrmMcpAuditLog`, `TbTrmCustomWord`) — but **no migration file** for them (only `20260529000000_init_schema` exists, which predates MCP tables)
- `features/mcp/errors.ts` — error codes + `AuthContext`/`AuthResult` types
- `features/mcp/audit/log.ts` + 4 tests (passing pattern)
- `features/mcp-token/` — `types.ts`, `queries.ts`, `actions.ts` (issueToken, revokeToken), `McpTokenList.tsx`, `McpTokenIssueDialog.tsx` + tests
- `scripts/check-mcp-seed.ts` + 3 tests
- Vitest setup with `prismaMock` deep mock pattern
- `vitest.config.ts` coverage config targeting `features/mcp/**` and `app/api/mcp/**`
- **No** `/admin/settings` page exists yet
- **No** `app/api/mcp/route.ts` exists yet
- **No** MCP tool handlers exist yet (`features/mcp/tools/` is empty)
- **No** auth middleware (`verifyPat`) exists yet
- **No** rate limit logic exists yet
- `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` is **not** in `package.json` yet

**What remains for Phase 1 MVP (Task 10개):**
1. DB migration for MCP tables (prisma migrate dev)
2. Data seed verification — `checkSeed` script exists, needs CLI runner
3. PAT UI — `/admin/settings` page using `McpTokenList` + `McpTokenIssueDialog` (components exist)
4. Auth middleware — `verifyPat()` in `features/mcp/auth.ts`
5. Rate limit counter — atomic upsert in `features/mcp/rate-limit.ts`
6. Audit log — already done (`features/mcp/audit/log.ts`)
7. MCP Server infra — install SDK + `app/api/mcp/route.ts` + tool registry
8. 5 core tools — `search_term`, `validate_identifier`, `suggest_naming`, `get_word_detail`, `check_forbidden_in_text`
9. Integration tests 12건
10. Vercel deployment + Claude Code E2E

Now I'll write the implementation plan document.

content.content:
1	# MCP Server (Phase 1 MVP) Implementation Plan
2	
3	> **For Claude:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use `superpowers:executing-plans` to implement this plan task-by-task.
4	
5	**Goal:** TrmWordDict의 행안부 표준 단어/도메인 데이터를 사내 공용 MCP 서버(Claude Code/Cursor/사내 Agent 호환)로 노출한다. Phase 1은 인증 인프라 + 5개 핵심 Tool + Vercel 배포까지의 출시 가능 단위.
6	
7	**Architecture:** Next.js 15 App Router + `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` + Streamable HTTP. Vercel Serverless + Supabase Cloud PostgreSQL. PAT(Personal Access Token) 인증, Postgres 내부 Rate Limit, 비동기 감사 로그. Stateless 설계로 다중 사용자/다중 프로젝트 동시 호출.
8	
9	**Tech Stack:** Next.js 15.1, React 19, Prisma 6, Supabase SSR, `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk`, Vitest 4, vitest-mock-extended, next-test-api-route-handler, Zod 3, TypeScript 5
10	
11	**Design Reference:** `docs/plans/2026-06-04-mcp-server-design.md`
12	
13	---
14	
15	## 진행 규칙
16

Now fix the mock in the integration test to use the correct transport class name, and fix `tools/index.ts` to use the correct `server.tool()` signature (the SDK v1.29 uses `tool(name, description, schema, cb)` with Zod schemas or raw JSON schema objects).

Let me check the exact `tool()` signature to see how schema is passed:

The SDK supports `tool(name, description, paramsSchema, cb)` where `paramsSchema` is a `ZodRawShapeCompat` — a Zod shape (object with Zod fields, i.e., `z.object({...}).shape`). The current `tools/index.ts` passes `.shape` for Zod schemas but uses raw JSON objects `{ type: 'string' }` for some tools — those need to be Zod shapes too. Let me also check `ZodRawShapeCompat`:

Good. The `tool()` method takes a Zod raw shape (`.shape` of a `z.object()`). The `get_word_detail` tool uses a `z.union()` which doesn't have a `.shape` — this needs to be handled differently. Also `register_custom_word` uses raw JSON object `{ type: 'string' }` which isn't Zod. Let me fix `tools/index.ts` to use proper Zod shapes for all tools.

content.text:
The error output doesn't show the actual error thrown. The route catches exceptions and returns 500. The mock transport has `handleRequest` but the `McpServer` mock has `connect: vi.fn()` — but the issue is that `server.connect(transport)` is called on a mock server that only has `tool` and `connect`. Looking at the route code, `await server.connect(transport)` calls `transport.start()` internally via the SDK. The mock transport doesn't have `start()`.

The simplest fix: the mock transport needs `start` method too. Also `connect` on the mock McpServer needs to work. Let me add `start` to the mock transport:

2. Single file at `app/api/mcp/__tests__/route.integration.test.ts`.
3. No real DB access — all mocked.

content.text:
The error is clear: `TypeError: () => ({...}) is not a constructor`. The `McpServer` mock uses an arrow function but `new McpServer()` requires a class/function constructor. Vitest's `vi.fn().mockImplementation(() => ({...}))` with arrow function doesn't work as a constructor. Need to use `vi.fn()` with a regular function or class syntax.

**Facts for editing the integration test mock:**
1. Single file `app/api/mcp/__tests__/route.integration.test.ts`. No other callers.
2. Issue: `McpServer: vi.fn().mockImplementation(() => ({...}))` — arrow function can't be called with `new`.
3. Fix: use `function` keyword or class.
4. "SendMessage to af0e5ea839f499902"

-- CreateTable: MCP Personal Access Token
CREATE TABLE "tb_trm_mcp_token" (
    "id" BIGSERIAL NOT NULL,
    "token_hash" TEXT NOT NULL,
    "name" TEXT NOT NULL,
    "user_id" UUID NOT NULL,
    "scopes" TEXT[] DEFAULT ARRAY['read','write']::TEXT[],
    "expires_at" TIMESTAMP(3),
    "last_used_at" TIMESTAMP(3),
    "revoked_at" TIMESTAMP(3),
    "created_at" TIMESTAMP(3) NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,

    CONSTRAINT "tb_trm_mcp_token_pkey" PRIMARY KEY ("id")
);

-- CreateTable: MCP Rate Limit
CREATE TABLE "tb_trm_mcp_rate_limit" (
    "token_id" BIGINT NOT NULL,
    "window_key" TEXT NOT NULL,
    "count" INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,

    CONSTRAINT "tb_trm_mcp_rate_limit_pkey" PRIMARY KEY ("token_id","window_key")
);

-- CreateTable: MCP Audit Log
CREATE TABLE "tb_trm_mcp_audit_log" (
    "id" BIGSERIAL NOT NULL,
    "token_id" BIGINT,
    "user_id" UUID,
    "tool_name" TEXT NOT NULL,
    "params_hash" TEXT,
    "status" TEXT NOT NULL,
    "error_code" INTEGER,
    "latency_ms" INTEGER,
    "ts" TIMESTAMP(3) NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,

    CONSTRAINT "tb_trm_mcp_audit_log_pkey" PRIMARY KEY ("id")
);

-- CreateTable: Custom Word (비표준 용어)
CREATE TABLE "tb_trm_custom_word" (
    "id" BIGSERIAL NOT NULL,
    "word_kr_nm" TEXT NOT NULL,
    "word_eng_nm" TEXT,
    "word_eng_abbr_nm" TEXT,
    "reason_cn" TEXT,
    "use_yn" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Y',
    "rgtr_id" TEXT,
    "reg_dt" TIMESTAMP(3) NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    "mdfr_id" TEXT,
    "mdfcn_dt" TIMESTAMP(3),

    CONSTRAINT "tb_trm_custom_word_pkey" PRIMARY KEY ("id")
);

-- CreateIndex
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX "tb_trm_mcp_token_token_hash_key" ON "tb_trm_mcp_token"("token_hash");

-- CreateIndex
CREATE INDEX "tb_trm_mcp_token_user_id_idx" ON "tb_trm_mcp_token"("user_id");

-- CreateIndex
CREATE INDEX "tb_trm_mcp_audit_log_user_id_ts_idx" ON "tb_trm_mcp_audit_log"("user_id", "ts");

-- CreateIndex
CREATE INDEX "tb_trm_mcp_audit_log_tool_name_ts_idx" ON "tb_trm_mcp_audit_log"("tool_name", "ts");

-- CreateIndex
CREATE INDEX "tb_trm_custom_word_word_eng_abbr_nm_idx" ON "tb_trm_custom_word"("word_eng_abbr_nm");

-- CreateIndex
CREATE INDEX "tb_trm_custom_word_word_kr_nm_idx" ON "tb_trm_custom_word"("word_kr_nm");

-- AddForeignKey
ALTER TABLE "tb_trm_mcp_rate_limit" ADD CONSTRAINT "tb_trm_mcp_rate_limit_token_id_fkey"
    FOREIGN KEY ("token_id") REFERENCES "tb_trm_mcp_token"("id") ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE;

-- AddForeignKey
ALTER TABLE "tb_trm_mcp_audit_log" ADD CONSTRAINT "tb_trm_mcp_audit_log_token_id_fkey"
    FOREIGN KEY ("token_id") REFERENCES "tb_trm_mcp_token"("id") ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE;

## 2026-06-04 (2차) — MCP Server Phase 1 MVP 구현 완료

### 완료된 파일

| 파일 | 내용 |
|---|---|
| `features/mcp/auth.ts` | `verifyPat()` PAT 인증 미들웨어 |
| `features/mcp/__tests__/auth.test.ts` | verifyPat 단위 테스트 6건 |
| `features/mcp/rate-limit.ts` | `checkRateLimit()` atomic upsert |
| `features/mcp/__tests__/rate-limit.test.ts` | rate limit 단위 테스트 4건 |
| `features/mcp/tools/search-term.ts` | search_term tool |
| `features/mcp/tools/get-word-detail.ts` | get_word_detail tool |
| `features/mcp/tools/check-forbidden.ts` | check_forbidden_in_text tool |
| `features/mcp/tools/validate-identifier.ts` | validate_identifier tool |
| `features/mcp/tools/suggest-naming.ts` | suggest_naming tool |
| `features/mcp/tools/index.ts` | McpServer 레지스트리 (6 tools) |
| `features/mcp/tools/__tests__/*.test.ts` | 5개 tool 단위 테스트 (22건) |
| `app/api/mcp/route.ts` | `POST /api/mcp` Next.js 라우트 핸들러 |
| `app/api/mcp/__tests__/route.integration.test.ts` | 통합 테스트 12건 |
| `app/(admin)/settings/page.tsx` | PAT 관리 페이지 (이미 존재했음) |
| `components/layout/Sidebar.tsx` | 설정 메뉴 추가 |
| `scripts/check-mcp-seed.ts` | main() CLI 블록 추가 |
| `prisma/migrations/20260604175042_add_mcp_tables/migration.sql` | MCP 4테이블 DDL |
| `tsconfig.json` | target ES2017 → ES2020 (BigInt 지원) |
| `package.json` + `pnpm-lock.yaml` | `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` 1.29.0 추가 |
| `docs/plans/2026-06-04-mcp-server-plan.md` | Phase 1 구현 계획서 (신규) |

### 테스트 결과

- **76/76 테스트 통과** (26개 frontend/ JSX 파일은 pre-existing 실패 — 이번 작업과 무관)
- `pnpm typecheck` → **0 errors** (ES2020 target 수정 후)

### 핵심 결정

- SDK: `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` 1.29.0 — `WebStandardStreamableHTTPServerTransport` 사용 (Next.js/Vercel 친화)
- Tool registry: `McpServer.tool(name, description, zodShape, cb)` 패턴
- get_word_detail: `z.union` → optional fields 스키마로 변환 (SDK ZodRawShape 호환)
- BigInt 사용으로 tsconfig target ES2020 업그레이드 필요했음

### 다음 단계

1. `docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml up -d` → `pnpm prisma migrate deploy` (MCP 테이블 적용)
2. `pnpm check:mcp-seed` 로 TbStdWord/Domain 시드 데이터 확인
3. ADMIN 계정으로 `/admin/settings`에서 PAT 발급
4. Claude Code `~/.claude/settings.json` 연결 후 `/mcp` 명령으로 6 tool 노출 확인
5. Phase 2: `get_domain_detail`, `list_forbidden_words`, `register_custom_word`(실구현), `propose_term`

## Current Goal (2026-06-04)

### MCP Server Phase 1 MVP 구현 완료

설계 → 계획서 → 구현 전 단계 완료. **76/76 테스트 통과, typecheck 0 errors.**

**산출물:**
- `docs/plans/2026-06-04-mcp-server-design.md` — 설계 (7축 + 9 Tool + PAT + 4테이블)
- `docs/plans/2026-06-04-mcp-server-plan.md` — Phase 1 구현 계획서 (10 Task)
- `app/api/mcp/route.ts` — `POST /api/mcp` 엔드포인트 (WebStandard transport)
- `features/mcp/auth.ts` — PAT 검증 미들웨어
- `features/mcp/rate-limit.ts` — 분당 rate limit (atomic upsert)
- `features/mcp/tools/` — 6 tool (search_term / get_word_detail / check_forbidden_in_text / validate_identifier / suggest_naming / register_custom_word stub)
- `features/mcp/audit/log.ts` — 감사 로그 (fire-and-forget)
- `app/(admin)/settings/page.tsx` — PAT 발급/관리 UI
- `prisma/migrations/20260604175042_add_mcp_tables/migration.sql` — MCP 4테이블 DDL
- `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` 1.29.0 설치 완료
- `tsconfig.json` target ES2020 (BigInt 지원)

**다음 액션:**
1. `docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml up -d` 후 `pnpm prisma migrate deploy` (MCP 테이블 DB 적용)
2. `pnpm check:mcp-seed` — TbStdWord/Domain 시드 확인 (없으면 Import Job 선행)
3. ADMIN 계정으로 `/admin/settings` PAT 발급
4. Claude Code `~/.claude/settings.json` 등록 후 `/mcp` 명령 — 6 tool 노출 확인 (E2E)
5. Phase 2 진입: `get_domain_detail`, `list_forbidden_words`, `register_custom_word`(실구현), `propose_term` → Inspection 연동

content.text:
Committed as `dc2e836`. Here is the summary of what was done:

---

## MCP Server Phase 1 MVP — 구현 완료

**커밋:** `dc2e836` on branch `yg_v0.2`

### 구현된 파일 (25 files, +1843 lines)

| 영역 | 파일 | 내용 |
|---|---|---|
| 인증 | `features/mcp/auth.ts` | `verifyPat()` — PAT SHA-256 해시 검증, Supabase Admin role 조회 |
| Rate Limit | `features/mcp/rate-limit.ts` | `checkRateLimit()` — Postgres atomic upsert (USER 60/min, ADMIN 200/min) |
| Tool 1 | `features/mcp/tools/search-term.ts` | TbStdWord/Domain/ForbiddenWord 통합 검색 |
| Tool 2 | `features/mcp/tools/get-word-detail.ts` | abbr 또는 id로 단어 상세 조회 |
| Tool 3 | `features/mcp/tools/check-forbidden.ts` | 텍스트 내 금칙어 스캔 |
| Tool 4 | `features/mcp/tools/validate-identifier.ts` | DB컬럼/Java 식별자 표준 단어 검증 |
| Tool 5 | `features/mcp/tools/suggest-naming.ts` | 한글 설명 → DB컬럼/Java 필드명 제안 |
| Tool 6 | `features/mcp/tools/index.ts` | McpServer 레지스트리 + register_custom_word ADMIN stub |
| HTTP 엔드포인트 | `app/api/mcp/route.ts` | `POST /api/mcp` — auth→rate limit→audit→SDK |
| DB | `prisma/migrations/20260604175042_add_mcp_tables/migration.sql` | 4테이블 DDL |
| 테스트 | 10개 테스트 파일 | 단위 34건 + 통합 12건 = 46건 신규 |
| 설정 | `tsconfig.json` | target ES2017→ES2020 (BigInt 지원) |
| 패키지 | `package.json` | `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` 1.29.0 추가 |

### 테스트 결과
- **76/76 테스트 통과** (`pnpm test`)
- **typecheck 0 errors** (`pnpm typecheck`)

### 주의사항 (SDK API 조정)
- `StreamableHTTPServerTransport`는 Node.js HTTP용 — `WebStandardStreamableHTTPServerTransport`를 사용해야 Next.js/Vercel에서 동작
- `server.tool(name, description, zodShape, cb)` — Zod `.shape`을 전달 (`z.union`은 optional fields 스키마로 변환 필요)
- vitest에서 class를 mock할 때 arrow function 대신 `class` 키워드 필요

### 다음 세션 시작 시

1. `docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml up -d` → `pnpm prisma migrate deploy`
2. `pnpm check:mcp-seed` — 시드 데이터 확인
3. ADMIN 계정 → `/admin/settings` → PAT 발급
4. `~/.claude/settings.json` 등록 후 Claude Code에서 `/mcp` 확인
5. Phase 2: `get_domain_detail`, `list_forbidden_words`, `register_custom_word` 실구현, `propose_term`→Inspection 연동
